profile.description: Memphis-headquartered truckload carrier founded May 5, 1985 by Ed Gatlin ('Big Ed'). Family-owned: Tim Gatlin (Chairman/CEO since 1989, co-owner since 1994) and the late Ed Gatlin (Founder). Todd Gatlin (VP Driver Relations) represents the next Gatlin generation in day-to-day operations. Fleet of 152 trucks and 133 drivers (FMCSA). Serves 48 states, Mexico, and Canada. Primary lane focus: East of I-35, South of I-80. HAZMAT specialist: 35% of loads are packaged chemicals (agricultural chemicals, paint-related materials, styrenes, polymers, chemical process industry products) including cyanide compounds. FedEx is a major client — Empire runs time-sensitive air cargo feeder lanes for FedEx in Memphis. Also hauls clothing/apparel, air cargo, home/office products, packaging, paper products, retail, electronics, cotton, general commodities. Dry van, no-touch freight. Revenue ~$18M (D&B). Certifications: American Chemistry Council Responsible Care partner, SmartWay Carrier, C-TPAT (Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism), International Cyanide Management Institute Certified Signatory. MCS-150 mileage: 13.2M miles (2022). Company philosophy: 'Treat People Right.' Port of Memphis member. Tim Gatlin serves on Tennessee Trucking Association board of directors. Memphis terminal at 3710 Tulane Road — trucks and trailers inspected by maintenance every time they roll through. Newer trucks have APUs and power inverters. USDOT 253615.
operating_regions: Primary focus: East of I-35, South of I-80
profile.corridor_alignment_notes: MAXIMUM GEOGRAPHIC ALIGNMENT. Empire Express is headquartered in Memphis, TN at 3710 Tulane Road — roughly 20 miles from the LHS Phase One site at the I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis, AR. Every truck in their 152-unit fleet originates from the Memphis terminal. Tim Gatlin lives in Collierville (east Memphis suburb). All CSV contacts have 901 area codes. Empire's HAZMAT/chemicals loads traverse the I-40/I-55 interchange heading west and south — those are the exact corridors where chemical plants and agricultural customers sit. Their Memphis terminal inspects every truck that rolls through — LHS flex-space truck terminal at the interchange extends that maintenance capability to the I-40 westbound corridor. FedEx air cargo feeder lanes originate from Memphis and run through the interchange. The I-40/I-55 interchange is squarely within Empire's primary lane focus (East of I-35, South of I-80).

classification.notes: Clear carrier model. NAICS 484121 matches. 152 trucks, asset-based truckload, HAZMAT, dry van. Family-owned. No brokerage division mentioned. Empire Logistics (Gene Phillips) is a separate entity — do not conflate with carrier operations.

fit_score.rationale: GEOGRAPHY (10.0): PERFECT SCORE. Empire Express is headquartered in Memphis at 3710 Tulane Road, ~20 miles from the LHS West Memphis site. Every truck originates from the Memphis terminal. All contacts are Memphis metro. This is a home-field carrier. I-40/I-55 is squarely within their primary lane focus (East of I-35, South of I-80). FLEET SCALE (8.0): 152 trucks, 133 drivers per FMCSA. Solidly in Dedicated Proprietary territory (100+ trucks). HAZMAT/chemicals specialization adds value — these are premium loads requiring specialized handling and safety infrastructure. 13.2M miles annually shows this fleet runs hard. OPERATING HISTORY (9.0): Founded 1985 — 40+ years. Ed Gatlin started the company to save a group of loyal drivers from a corporate fleet rejection. Tim Gatlin has been CEO since 1989 — 36 years running the company. MBA from Ole Miss, former bank lending officer. This is a deeply rooted Memphis institution with generational continuity. CONTACT QUALITY (9.5): UPGRADED from 8.0. Website scrape revealed a full VP-level leadership team — 8 VP/President contacts plus directors, vs. 4 contacts in original Lusha CSV. Direct access to Tim Gatlin (CEO), John Phillips (COO), Mark Hopper (VP Safety), Danny Walston (VP Maintenance), Don Stroud (VP Sales), Rod Hudson (VP Driver Ops), plus Jera Skaggs (Director Recruiting) and Todd Gatlin (VP Driver Relations — family). Multi-threaded approach is now fully viable. STRATEGIC SIGNALS (10.0): PERFECT SCORE. FedEx is a confirmed major client (air cargo feeder lanes) — LHS at the I-40 interchange directly supports those lanes. C-TPAT and ICMI certifications confirm elite safety culture. 'Treat People Right' founding philosophy = total alignment with LHS driver-first mission. TTA board. Port of Memphis. American Chemistry Council Responsible Care. The philosophical alignment is total.
fit_score.version_note: Score upgraded from 9.0 to 9.2 on dossier v2 — contact quality upgrade driven by full leadership team discovery.

contacts.name: Tim Gatlin
contacts.title: Chairman and Chief Executive Officer / Co-Owner
contacts.strategic_angle: FOUNDER-LEVEL CONTACT. Tim Gatlin is the Chairman/CEO and co-owner of Empire Express. He joined the company in January 1989, became CEO two months later at age ~25, and purchased half the stock in 1994. MBA from Ole Miss. Former lending officer at Trustmark Bank (Columbus MS) — he understands finance, capital allocation, and business case analysis. Serves on the Tennessee Trucking Association board alongside other Memphis carrier leaders. Tim has been running Empire for 36 years. His father Ed started the company to save a group of drivers. Tim continues the 'Treat People Right' philosophy. The Gatlin family was honored by Carnival Memphis with the Chairman's Award at their Business & Industry Salute Luncheon — Tim is a Memphis civic figure, not just a carrier CEO. Jeff approaches as a peer: builder-to-builder, Memphis neighbor-to-neighbor, driver-first-to-driver-first. Tim lives in Collierville (affluent east Memphis suburb, ~30 miles from LHS site). This is Jeff's conversation from Day 1.
contacts.personality_read: Collierville TN. MBA, former banker turned trucking CEO at age ~25. 36 years running the family company. TTA board member. Carnival Memphis Chairman's Award recipient. Philosophy: 'Treat People Right.' Analytical (banking background) but values-driven (father's legacy). Likely conservative, disciplined, family-oriented. Small LinkedIn presence (3 connections) suggests he doesn't live online — he's running trucks.

enrichment.bio_summary: Chairman/CEO and co-owner of Empire Express since 1989. MBA from University of Mississippi. Former lending officer at Trustmark Bank. Purchased half the company from his father Ed Gatlin in 1994. TTA board member. Port of Memphis member. Carnival Memphis Chairman's Award recipient. 36 years leading the company. Lives in Collierville, TN.

Memphis neighbor: 'We're 20 miles from Tulane Road, building at the I-40/I-55 interchange.'

enrichment.approach_summary: Tim Gatlin is the owner, the CEO, and the soul of Empire Express. His father started the company to save a group of drivers. Tim has spent 36 years honoring that legacy. He's on the TTA board — he knows every carrier in Memphis. He was honored by Carnival Memphis as a civic leader. Jeff approaches with respect for the legacy, references the driver-first philosophy, and invites Tim to the founding table. This is not a sales pitch. It's an invitation from one Memphis builder to another. Tim's banking background means he'll evaluate the economics rigorously — have the Carrier Calculator ready. But the emotional hook is the legacy: 'Your dad started this company for drivers. We're building infrastructure for the same reason.'

contacts.name: Rod Hudson
contacts.title: Vice President of Driver Operations
contacts.strategic_angle: ANCHOR OPERATIONAL CONTACT. VP of DRIVER Operations — not fleet ops, not general ops, DRIVER operations. At a company whose philosophy is 'Treat People Right' and whose founding story is about saving drivers, the VP of Driver Operations is the person who executes that philosophy every day. LHS flex-space truck terminals are driver infrastructure. Rod Hudson IS the audience. Lead with: 'Rod — Empire's philosophy is to treat people right. What would it mean for your drivers to have access to flex-space truck terminal infrastructure at the I-40/I-55 interchange instead of truck stops?' 708 area code = Chicago origin. May have relocated to Memphis for the role. NOTE: ZoomInfo also shows a separate 'Director of Driver Operations' title held by Michelle Tetrick — Rod is VP above her.
contacts.personality_read: Memphis-based per CSV. 708 area code (south suburbs of Chicago) suggests relocated. VP title at a 152-truck carrier = significant authority. 'Driver Operations' focus = driver advocate, quality-of-life champion.

enrichment.bio_summary: VP of Driver Operations at Empire Express. Confirmed on company website. 708 area code suggests Chicago-area origin, relocated to Memphis. Leads driver experience and operations reporting to Tim Gatlin.

Website-confirmed role = active, not stale Lusha data

In multi-threaded outreach, reaching Phillips simultaneously with Tim adds pressure and legitimacy: when Tim hears about LHS from Jeff, and then hears Phillips is already in conversation with JJ, the pitch gains internal momentum. Empire hauls cyanide compounds, agricultural poisons, styrenes, and polymers. Mark Hopper owns the safety philosophy that keeps those loads moving without incident. LHS flex-space terminals speak directly to his mandate: secure HAZMAT parking, professional maintenance for loaded chemical trailers, staging that meets Responsible Care requirements. If Hopper endorses LHS internally, it removes the HAZMAT objection before Tim ever raises it. This is a pre-emption contact. Approach before Tim's HAZMAT question becomes an objection.
contacts.personality_read: VP Safety at a certified Responsible Care carrier hauling cyanide and agricultural chemicals. Process-oriented, risk-aware, regulation-fluent. The ACC Responsible Care program and ICMI certification require continuous improvement documentation — he thinks in systems. Will respond to infrastructure that matches the safety culture he's built.

Jeff approaches — never JJ. Reference Ed's legacy. Todd lives this every day.

strategic_positioning.win_thesis: Empire Express is a 152-truck, 40-year Memphis carrier founded by Ed Gatlin to save a group of drivers that a corporation threw away. Tim Gatlin has run the company for 36 years on the philosophy 'Treat People Right.' They're headquartered 20 miles from the LHS Phase One site, every truck in their fleet originates from Memphis, and their CEO sits on the Tennessee Trucking Association board. They hold Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI Certified Signatory status — among the most rigorous safety certifications a carrier can hold. FedEx is a major client. LHS flex-space truck terminals are built on the same principle Empire was founded on: infrastructure that treats drivers the way they deserve. If Mid South Transport (CAR-0008) is the local validation story, Empire Express is the values validation story. Two Memphis carriers whose founding philosophies align perfectly with LHS — that's the narrative that sells every subsequent carrier.

pain_points.pain: HAZMAT driver safety and rest quality on the road
pain_points.detail: 35% of Empire's loads are packaged chemicals — including cyanide compounds (ICMI certification). HAZMAT drivers face higher regulatory scrutiny, stricter HOS enforcement, and greater safety stakes. When a HAZMAT driver is fatigued at a truck stop, the consequences are categorically worse than a dry van incident. Quality rest infrastructure is a safety imperative. LHS flex-space terminals provide professional, secure staging for HAZMAT-loaded trucks — aligned with Responsible Care commitments.

pain_points.pain: Driver recruiting and retention in a shortage market
pain_points.detail: Ed Gatlin himself named recruiting spend as a major pain: 'We spend a large amount of money, like most truck lines, recruiting.' Empire has a VP of Driver Operations, a Director of Driver Operations, a Director of Recruiting, and a VP of Driver Relations (Todd Gatlin). The organizational investment in driver culture is real — but at 152 trucks they compete against mega-fleets with bigger budgets. LHS founding membership becomes a recruiting differentiator on driveforempire.com and in recruiter pitches: 'Join Empire — your drivers get dedicated terminal infrastructure at the I-40/I-55 interchange.'

pain_points.pain: Fleet maintenance away from Memphis terminal
pain_points.detail: Empire's Memphis terminal inspects every truck that rolls through (confirmed by driver reviews). VP of Maintenance Danny Walston owns this standard. But when trucks are on I-40 heading west through West Memphis, maintenance means a truck stop or roadside service. For a HAZMAT-loaded truck, a breakdown is a safety event, not just a delay. LHS flex-space terminal at the interchange extends the Tulane Road maintenance standard 20 miles west.

FedEx is a confirmed major client for Empire's air cargo feeder lanes. Those loads have zero tolerance for delay. LHS at the I-40/I-55 interchange — the gateway interchange for westbound Memphis freight — provides staging and relay capability that tightens time-sensitive lane performance. Don Stroud (VP Sales) and John Phillips (COO) will immediately understand this framing.

pain_points.pain: Mid-size carrier competing for premium HAZMAT customers
pain_points.detail: As an ACC Responsible Care member with C-TPAT and ICMI certifications, Empire competes for premium chemical shipper accounts against larger HAZMAT carriers. Founding membership in LHS = infrastructure story that demonstrates commitment to driver safety and operational excellence beyond what competitors offer. Don Stroud can use this in customer conversations.

strategic_positioning.competitive_angle: Empire Express was literally founded to take care of drivers. Big Ed started the company because he wouldn't let loyal drivers down. Forty years later, Tim carries that torch — and his family's name is on the VP of Driver Relations title too. LHS flex-space truck terminals extend that philosophy from the Memphis terminal to the road. When Empire's HAZMAT drivers are 300 miles down I-40 hauling cyanide compounds, they deserve better than a truck stop. They deserve infrastructure built with the same 'Treat People Right' philosophy that Empire was founded on. A company with Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI certifications shouldn't be staging at Pilot. That's what LHS is.
leverage_points: Memphis HQ — 20 miles from LHS Phase One. Another local champion.
'Treat People Right' founding philosophy = total alignment with LHS driver-first mission.
Tim Gatlin: 36-year CEO, TTA board member, MBA, co-owner. Carnival Memphis Chairman's Award recipient.
HAZMAT specialist with ICMI Certified Signatory status — highest HAZMAT tier for cyanide compounds.
Responsible Care + C-TPAT + ICMI = elite safety culture that demands matching infrastructure.
FedEx is a major client — time-sensitive lane performance tied to I-40 interchange.
Port of Memphis member = embedded in Memphis logistics community.
TTA board = Tim knows every carrier CEO in Tennessee personally.
Ed Gatlin origin story: saved a group of drivers from corporate abandonment. LHS saves drivers from truck stop dependency.
Todd Gatlin (VP Driver Relations) carries the family legacy in his daily title — internal champion candidate.
Active driver shortage pain — Ed Gatlin confirmed recruiting spend as a major cost. LHS is a recruiting tool.
driveforempire.com = active investment in driver recruiting brand. LHS membership upgrades that brand.
VP of DRIVER Operations (not fleet ops) = driver-centric org structure with a full team under him.
Paired with Mid South Transport (CAR-0008): two Memphis carriers as founding members = unassailable local validation.
Full VP leadership team now accessible — 10 contacts across CEO, COO, 5 VPs, director, family, fleet manager.

objection_map.objection: We're a HAZMAT carrier — does your facility handle HAZMAT?
objection_map.response: LHS flex-space truck terminals are designed for professional carrier operations, including HAZMAT-loaded equipment. Secure parking for loaded trailers, professional maintenance that understands HAZMAT equipment, and staging areas that meet the standards your Responsible Care program, C-TPAT certification, and ICMI signatory status require. Empire's drivers hauling cyanide compounds and agricultural chemicals deserve infrastructure that matches the seriousness of what they're carrying. That's not a truck stop.

objection_map.objection: Our Memphis terminal already takes care of our drivers
objection_map.response: It does — Danny Walston's maintenance culture and your inspection discipline at Tulane Road are part of why Empire's safety record is what it is. But when your driver is 300 miles down I-40 heading west, what does he have? A truck stop. LHS flex-space truck terminal at the I-40/I-55 interchange — 20 miles from Tulane Road — extends that Tulane Road standard to the first major interchange west of your yard.

80% refundable = $20K net. Flex Proprietary (reserved days) is also available if you want to start smaller. And the Finance Option spreads Dedicated to $10K/month. But here's the real math: what does one HAZMAT driver turnover cost you? Ed Gatlin said recruiting is a major spend. One replacement = $12K-$15K. LHS as a recruiting and retention tool saves that in the first year. And at $13/day, it costs less than a truck stop stay.

objection_map.objection: Who else is signing up? I know everyone in Memphis trucking.
objection_map.response: That's exactly why we want you at the table, Tim. You know the Memphis carriers. You sit on the TTA board. When we bring the Memphis trucking community together as founding members, your voice carries weight. We're building this for the carriers you've been working alongside for 40 years.

objection_map.objection: Our drivers mostly run East of I-35 and South of I-80 — does this interchange help us?
objection_map.response: The I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis is the eastern anchor of your westbound and southbound lanes. Every driver leaving Memphis on I-40 heading toward your chemical plant customers in Arkansas, Louisiana, and Texas passes through that interchange. It's not a detour — it's mile one. And your FedEx air cargo lanes originate from Memphis — staging at the interchange tightens those time windows.

first_touch.sender: Jeff to Tim Gatlin (CEO). JJ to Rod Hudson (VP Driver Ops, phone only). JJ emails John Phillips (COO) and Mark Hopper (VP Safety) on Day 2.
first_touch.angle_summary: This is a Memphis family conversation. Jeff approaches Tim as a peer builder — founder-to-founder, Memphis neighbor-to-neighbor. Reference Ed Gatlin's origin story, the 'Treat People Right' philosophy, and the LHS flex-space truck terminal vision. JJ approaches Rod Hudson with the driver operations angle. JJ follows Day 2 with the COO (Phillips), Safety VP (Hopper), and Maintenance VP (Walston) — simultaneous threads before Tim has responded, so when Tim asks his team, they're already in conversation.
first_touch.tone: Personal, respectful of legacy, Memphis-proud. Reference Tulane Road, the interchange, the philosophy. This should feel like a conversation between Memphis trucking families, not a corporate sales call.

sequence_recommendation.action: Source Rod Hudson email — critical path (VP Driver Ops, no email in CSV). Source Jera Skaggs email via LinkedIn. Verify John Phillips, Mark Hopper, Danny Walston, Don Stroud emails via LinkedIn (first-name pattern high confidence). Verify Tommy Robinson location (Kansas vs Memphis) via LinkedIn. Prepare HAZMAT facility talking points — Hopper will vet LHS before Tim commits. Coordinate timing with Mid South Transport (CAR-0008) — approach both Memphis carriers same week. Review TTA board calendar — next Memphis regional meeting, Jeff should attend.

Jeff emails Tim Gatlin — founder-to-founder, 'Treat People Right' alignment, flex-space truck terminal vision at I-40 interchange, 20 miles from Tulane Road. Reference Ed Gatlin legacy. Reference Carnival Memphis civic recognition. DRAFT-001. JJ calls Rod Hudson mobile — VP Driver Ops, driver infrastructure angle. No email available. Use DRAFT-002 talking points. If Hudson picks up: pitch flex-space terminal, ask for email.

JJ emails John Phillips (COO) — operational scale angle, Dedicated Proprietary economics for 152-truck fleet. DRAFT-003. JJ emails Mark Hopper (VP Safety) — HAZMAT, Responsible Care, C-TPAT, ICMI alignment. DRAFT-004. JJ emails Danny Walston (VP Maintenance) — Tulane Road standard extension, maintenance touchpoint at interchange. DRAFT-005. JJ emails Jonathan Gaines (Director Logistics) — routing and staging angle. DRAFT-006. JJ emails Tommy Robinson (Fleet Manager) — fleet maintenance angle. DRAFT-007. If Hudson email sourced: JJ emails Hudson. DRAFT-008.

Jeff calls Tim Gatlin if Day 1 email got no response. Primary: +1 901-647-7954. Alt: +1 901-251-1211. Memphis call — warm and direct. Reference Ed Gatlin legacy. JJ emails Don Stroud (VP Sales) — competitive differentiation, FedEx lane performance angle. DRAFT-009. JJ emails Jera Skaggs (Recruiting Director) — driver recruiting differentiator, turnover ROI angle. DRAFT-010. JJ follows up Rod Hudson call if Day 1 connected but no meeting set.

If no response from any channel: Jeff and JJ drive to 3710 Tulane Road with site plans. 20 miles from LHS site. Same playbook as Mid South Transport — Memphis carriers get in-person attention. If Tim engaged: schedule Carrier Calculator demo and West Memphis site visit. Jeff warm-approaches Todd Gatlin only if Tim is unreachable after Day 5 — family side door, not cold pitch.

If engaged: site visit (20-minute drive from Tulane Road). Carrier Calculator demo for 152-truck HAZMAT fleet. Propose founding table meeting with other Memphis carriers (Mid South Transport, etc.). If Tim asks 'who else?': reference Mid South Transport (CAR-0008) and Memphis carrier founding table concept. If silence: maintain via TTA events. Tim Gatlin is at every TTA Memphis regional meeting — Jeff should be in the room.

playbook.escalation_trigger: At a 152-truck family carrier where the CEO is the co-owner, there is no escalation above Tim Gatlin. If Jeff's Day 1 email and Day 3 call get silence, the next move is in-person at Tulane Road or a TTA event. Todd Gatlin (VP Driver Relations, family) is the warm side door if Tim remains unreachable — approach only after Day 5 and only if Jeff leads the conversation.

approach_flags.jeff_should_enter_rationale: Tim Gatlin is the owner and 36-year CEO of a family business his father started for drivers. Jeff approaches as a peer. This is Jeff's conversation from Day 1. JJ handles the operational VP contacts.
approach_flags.video_outreach_rationale: A video from the LHS West Memphis site: 'Tim — this is 20 miles from Tulane Road. Here's what we're building for Memphis carriers like Empire Express.' Can also reference the interchange and HAZMAT corridor.
approach_flags.linkedin_tagging_rationale: Tim Gatlin has 3 LinkedIn connections. He's not on LinkedIn. Phone and email are the right channels. In-person is best.
approach_flags.in_person_visit_rationale: ABSOLUTELY. 3710 Tulane Road is 20 miles from the LHS site. Same playbook as Mid South Transport: drive to the yard, bring site plans, shake hands. If Tim is at the TTA Memphis regional meeting, Jeff should be in the room.
approach_flags.calculator_demo_rationale: Tim has an MBA and banking background. He'll want to see the math. 152-truck HAZMAT fleet, Dedicated Proprietary economics, turnover cost comparison. Have the Calculator ready for the first meeting.
approach_flags.pricing_in_first_touch_rationale: Carrier model. Include Dedicated Proprietary pricing. 152 trucks is clearly above the 100-truck threshold. Also mention Flex Proprietary as an alternative. Tim's banking brain will want numbers in the first conversation.
approach_flags.site_visit_rationale: 20 minutes from Tulane Road across the bridge. Invite Tim to drive over and walk the site. This is the highest-value action for a local carrier. If Tim sees the site in person, the conversation changes from theoretical to real.

tone_profile.company_tone: Family legacy. Driver-first. Memphis proud. 'Treat People Right' is the thread that connects Empire Express to LHS. Never pitch — invite. Reference Ed Gatlin's origin story. Reference the philosophy. Make Tim feel like this was built for carriers like his. Acknowledge Carnival Memphis civic recognition — Tim is not just a carrier CEO, he's a Memphis institution.
tone_profile.jj_variant: Direct, operational, role-specific. Each VP gets a different angle: Hopper gets safety/HAZMAT, Walston gets maintenance extension, Hudson gets driver culture, Phillips gets operational economics, Stroud gets competitive differentiation, Skaggs gets recruiting ROI. Never the same email twice.
tone_profile.jeff_variant: Peer founder. Memphis neighbor. 'Tim — your dad started Empire to take care of drivers that a corporation threw away. Forty years later, you're still living that philosophy — and your family is still running the company that carries his name. We're building flex-space truck terminals with the same conviction: infrastructure that treats drivers the way they deserve. I'd like to show you what we're putting at the I-40/I-55 interchange, 20 miles from Tulane Road. When can we meet?'

pre_send_flags.detail: No email in CSV. VP-level operational leader who owns driver experience. Phone available (+1 708-299-0417). 708 area code = south Chicago suburbs. Website confirms name is 'Rod' not 'Rodney' — dossier v1 had wrong first name.
pre_send_flags.required_action: Source email via LinkedIn. If email unavailable, JJ calls Hudson mobile on Day 1 — phone is the primary channel until email is sourced. Note name correction: Rod, not Rodney.

pre_send_flags.detail: Lusha CSV did not assign email confidence rating. Email jonathan@empireexpress.com follows first-name pattern (consistent with tim@, tommy@). Likely valid but unconfirmed.
pre_send_flags.required_action: Verify via LinkedIn before Day 2 send.

pre_send_flags.required_action: LinkedIn-verify location. Low risk — proceed with Memphis assumption.

Jeff approaches Todd only after Tim is warm or after Day 5 with no response. Email likely todd@empireexpress.com but do not send without warm context.

pre_send_flags.who_or_what: HAZMAT facility answer — must have ready
pre_send_flags.detail: Empire holds ACC Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI Certified Signatory status. They will ask if LHS handles HAZMAT-loaded equipment to their certification standards. This is not a maybe — it is a certainty. Mark Hopper (VP Safety) will vet LHS specifically. Must have clear answers on HAZMAT parking, containment standards, and Responsible Care alignment before any contact is initiated.
pre_send_flags.required_action: Develop HAZMAT facility talking points addressing: (1) secure HAZMAT-loaded trailer parking, (2) containment standards, (3) Responsible Care process compatibility, (4) C-TPAT chain-of-custody compatibility. Have these ready BEFORE Day 1.

pre_send_flags.who_or_what: Empire Logistics / Empire Financial — separate entities
pre_send_flags.detail: Gene Phillips (President of Empire Logistics) and Jackie Buckingham (President of Empire Financial) are listed on the leadership page as separate business units. Do not confuse with carrier operations. Jonathan Gaines (Director of Logistics) is an operational role within the carrier — not affiliated with Empire Logistics subsidiary. Do not pitch Gene Phillips or Jackie Buckingham on LHS — different business lines.
pre_send_flags.required_action: Exclude Gene Phillips and Jackie Buckingham from LHS outreach. Carrier contacts only.

offer_pricing.pricing_notes: 152 trucks is solidly above the 100-truck Dedicated Proprietary threshold. $100K initiation (80% refundable = $20K net) + $13/day. At ~$18M revenue, $100K is ~0.56% of annual revenue — significant but manageable, especially with 80% refundable. Tim Gatlin has an MBA and banking background — lead with the financial structure: refundability, net exposure, daily cost comparison to truck stop spend. Finance Option ($10K/mo × 12) available if cash flow timing matters. Flex Proprietary available if Tim wants to start smaller and scale. For a HAZMAT carrier with C-TPAT and ICMI certifications, frame the safety value: what does one HAZMAT incident cost? One driver turnover? LHS at $13/day is insurance, not expense.
offer_pricing.capex_comparison_notes: Empire operates one Memphis terminal at Tulane Road. LHS comparison: instead of building or leasing a second facility at the I-40 interchange, access flex-space truck terminal infrastructure for $100K (80% refundable) + $13/day. For Tim's MBA brain: traditional terminal $165K/space new. LHS: $100K, 80% refundable = $20K net = 88% reduction. For a HAZMAT carrier, the safety/compliance infrastructure at LHS supports their Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI commitments.

engagement.next_step: Resolve pre_send flags F-001 through F-009 (source emails, verify phones). Prepare HAZMAT facility answer (F-011). Coordinate timing with Mid South Transport (CAR-0008). Day 1: Jeff emails Tim, JJ calls Rod Hudson.

key_risks: VP DRIVER OPS HAS NO EMAIL: Rod Hudson (C-0002) is the anchor operational contact but has no email. JJ must call cold on Day 1 or source email first. Name corrected to 'Rod' from 'Rodney' per website.

HAZMAT FACILITY QUESTION IS CERTAIN: Empire holds Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI Certified Signatory status (cyanide compounds). Mark Hopper (VP Safety) will vet LHS specifically. HAZMAT talking points must be fully developed before any contact — not after Tim raises the question.

EMPIRE LOGISTICS CONFLICT RISK: Gene Phillips (President Empire Logistics) and Jackie Buckingham (President Empire Financial) appear on the leadership page but run separate subsidiaries. Pitching them would be off-target and confusing. Carrier contacts only.
REVENUE SCALE: ~$18M revenue means $100K is ~0.56% of annual revenue. The 80% refundable framing and Finance Option are important. Tim's banking background means he'll do the math — have it ready.

'WHO ELSE IS IN?': Tim Gatlin sits on the TTA board. He knows every carrier in Memphis. He will ask who else is committed. Having Mid South Transport (CAR-0008) at the table first — or approaching simultaneously — strengthens both conversations.
SMALL LINKEDIN: Tim has 3 LinkedIn connections. LinkedIn outreach is ineffective. Phone and in-person are the right channels.

LANE FOCUS OBJECTION: Empire focuses on lanes East of I-35, South of I-80. Have the I-40 westbound lane argument ready — I-40/I-55 is the eastern anchor of their westbound and southbound network, not a detour.
TODD GATLIN RISK: Do not cold-approach Todd Gatlin. He is family. Approaching without Tim's warmth could create a defensive dynamic. Jeff owns this relationship — warm only.
chip_assessment.close_path: 1) Resolve pre_send flags — source emails, prepare HAZMAT answer. 2) Jeff emails Tim Day 1 — founder-to-founder, 'Treat People Right' alignment, 20 miles from Tulane Road. JJ calls Rod Hudson Day 1. 3) Day 2: JJ emails John Phillips, Mark Hopper, Danny Walston, Jonathan Gaines, Tommy Robinson — simultaneous VP-level threads. 4) Day 3: Jeff calls Tim if no email response. JJ emails Don Stroud and Jera Skaggs. 5) Day 5: Jeff and JJ visit Tulane Road. 20 miles. Bring site plans. If Tim unreachable, Jeff warm-approaches Todd Gatlin. 6) If Tim engages: Carrier Calculator demo (152-truck HAZMAT fleet economics, FedEx lane performance), site visit invitation (20 minutes to West Memphis). 7) If Tim asks 'who else?': reference Mid South Transport (CAR-0008) and Memphis carrier founding table. 8) Target: in-person meeting within 10 days. Tim lives in Collierville.
chip_assessment.company_intel_summary: Empire Express is a 152-truck Memphis carrier founded in 1985 by Ed Gatlin to save a group of drivers. Tim Gatlin (Chairman/CEO, MBA, 36-year tenure, TTA board, Carnival Memphis Chairman's Award) runs the company on 'Treat People Right.' HAZMAT specialist hauling packaged chemicals including cyanide compounds — holds ACC Responsible Care, C-TPAT, and ICMI Certified Signatory status. FedEx is a major client for air cargo feeder lanes. Headquartered at 3710 Tulane Road, 20 miles from LHS Phase One. Full VP leadership team now mapped: COO, 5 VPs, recruiting director, Todd Gatlin (family/VP Driver Relations). 10 contacts total. This is the values alignment story.
chip_assessment.maverick_relevance: Empire Express paired with Mid South Transport creates the Memphis founding member narrative. Two Memphis carriers — one a 40-year HAZMAT specialist built to save drivers, the other a 43-year OTR carrier rooted in Memphis — both at the founding table. When Jeff pitches PAM Transport or Covenant Logistics, he says: 'The Memphis carriers are already in. Empire Express and Mid South Transport — both founded on driver-first philosophies, both headquartered within 20 miles of the site — are founding members.' That's not a sales pitch. That's a movement. Tim Gatlin sits on the TTA board alongside FedEx Freight (Rob Rhea), Intermodal Cartage (Mark George), Wooten Transports (Mike Lathum), and JNJ Express (Lester Lawson). If Tim endorses LHS, the word-of-mouth through TTA hits every major Memphis motor carrier at once.

drafts.subject: Empire Express and flex-space truck terminals at I-40/I-55
drafts.body:
Tim —

Your dad started Empire Express because he wouldn't let a group of loyal drivers down. Forty years later, you're still running the company on that principle — and your family is still running it alongside you. That's rare, and it's exactly why I wanted to reach out.

We're building a network of flex-space truck terminals — starting at the I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis, about 20 miles from Tulane Road. These aren't truck stops. They're shared-access terminal facilities where carriers like Empire get driver staging, professional maintenance, truck wash, and secure parking — without building or leasing fixed infrastructure.

For a 152-truck HAZMAT carrier running 48 states out of Memphis, this means your drivers get terminal-quality operations at the busiest freight interchange in the Mid-South. Dedicated Proprietary membership is $100,000 initiation — 80% refundable — plus $13 per day. That's less than most truck stop overnight stays, and your drivers get infrastructure that matches what you've built at Tulane Road.

I'd like to show you the site. It's a 20-minute drive from your yard. When can we meet?

Jeff
drafts.hook_summary: Ed Gatlin origin story + family legacy + 'Treat People Right' philosophy alignment
drafts.ask: Site visit — 20-minute drive from Tulane Road
drafts.notes: Jeff sends Day 1. Founder-to-founder. Reference Tulane Road proximity. Include pricing. Tim's MBA/banking background means he'll want numbers immediately. The emotional hook is the legacy; the close is the math.

drafts.body:
PHONE CALL TALKING POINTS — Rod Hudson, VP Driver Operations

Opening: 'Rod — I'm JJ with LineHaul Station. I know your title is VP of Driver Operations at Empire Express, and everything we're building is for the people you take care of every day. Do you have five minutes?'

Pitch: 'We're building flex-space truck terminals at key freight interchanges — starting at I-40/I-55 in West Memphis, about 20 miles from your Memphis terminal. These give your drivers terminal-quality staging, maintenance, truck wash, and secure rest — instead of truck stops. For your HAZMAT drivers especially, rest quality isn't a perk. It's a safety requirement.'

Ask: 'Can I send you a one-pager and set up 20 minutes to walk you through what this looks like for Empire's 152-truck fleet?'

If he asks about pricing: '$100,000 initiation, 80% refundable, plus $13 a day per truck. Founding member access. Less than a truck stop overnight.'

If he asks who else: 'We're working with Memphis carriers right now. I'd rather show you the specifics in person — the site is 20 minutes from Tulane Road.'

Get his email for follow-up.
drafts.hook_summary: VP of Driver Operations — everything LHS builds is for the people he manages
drafts.notes: PHONE ONLY — Rod has no email. JJ calls mobile +1 708-299-0417 Day 1. If voicemail: leave message referencing Empire Express by name and the I-40/I-55 interchange. Try again Day 3.

drafts.subject: Operational infrastructure for Empire Express at I-40/I-55
drafts.body:
John —

I'm reaching out because Empire Express runs 152 trucks and 13 million miles a year out of Memphis — and we're building something at the I-40/I-55 interchange that changes what operational infrastructure looks like for a fleet at your scale.

LineHaul Station is a flex-space truck terminal at the West Memphis interchange, 20 miles from Tulane Road. No construction, no lease — Dedicated Proprietary membership gives Empire professional maintenance, driver staging, truck wash, and secure HAZMAT parking at the first major interchange west of your yard. For a COO running 48-state operations out of a single terminal, that's a second quality touchpoint without the capital.

Dedicated Proprietary: $100,000 initiation (80% refundable) + $13/day. Worth 20 minutes to walk through how this works for Empire's operation?

JJ
drafts.hook_summary: COO operational infrastructure angle — 152 trucks, 13M miles, single terminal extending west
drafts.notes: JJ sends Day 2. COO angle — operational scale, no-CapEx infrastructure. Let Jeff's Day 1 Tim email land before this goes out.

drafts.subject: Responsible Care infrastructure — Empire Express at the I-40 interchange
drafts.body:
Mark —

Empire holds ACC Responsible Care certification, C-TPAT status, and ICMI Certified Signatory designation. That level of safety rigor is rare — and it deserves matching infrastructure when your drivers are away from Tulane Road.

We're building flex-space truck terminals at the I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis — 20 miles from your Memphis terminal. For Empire's HAZMAT operation, that means secure parking for loaded chemical trailers, professional maintenance for HAZMAT-equipped trucks, and staging that meets the standards your certifications require. When your drivers are hauling packaged chemicals and cyanide compounds down I-40, a truck stop is not Responsible Care.

I'd like to walk you through how LHS aligns with Empire's safety management system. Can we get 20 minutes?

JJ
drafts.hook_summary: HAZMAT certifications (Responsible Care + C-TPAT + ICMI) demand matching infrastructure away from Tulane Road
drafts.ask: 20-minute meeting — position as LHS safety review, not a pitch
drafts.notes: JJ sends Day 2. CRITICAL contact — Hopper is the HAZMAT objection pre-emption. If Hopper is engaged before Tim raises the HAZMAT question, JJ can tell Tim: 'Your VP of Safety is already reviewing LHS alignment with your Responsible Care commitments.' Have full HAZMAT facility talking points ready.

drafts.subject: Fleet maintenance at I-40/I-55 — extending the Tulane Road standard
drafts.body:
Danny —

I know Empire inspects every truck that rolls through the Memphis terminal. That maintenance discipline is a big part of why a 152-truck HAZMAT fleet runs 13 million miles a year safely.

We're building flex-space truck terminal infrastructure at the I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis — 20 miles from Tulane Road. For your fleet, that means professional maintenance, truck wash, and secure equipment parking at the first major interchange west of your yard. When your trucks are heading down I-40 and need service, the option is no longer a truck stop or turning back to Memphis.

For a HAZMAT fleet where a breakdown isn't just a delay — it's a safety event — having a maintenance-quality touchpoint 20 miles from your terminal changes the road call math.

Can we talk through what this looks like for Empire's maintenance operation?

JJ
drafts.hook_summary: Tulane Road inspection discipline + Walston's maintenance standard extended to I-40 corridor
drafts.ask: Call to discuss fleet maintenance impact
drafts.notes: JJ sends Day 2. Reference their known maintenance culture — Walston will know you've done your homework. HAZMAT road call angle is the differentiator vs. a generic maintenance pitch.

drafts.subject: Flex-space terminal at the I-40/I-55 interchange — Empire Express logistics
drafts.body:
Jonathan —

I'm reaching out because Empire Express runs freight through the I-40/I-55 interchange every day — and we're building something there that changes how Memphis carriers manage that corridor.

LineHaul Station is a flex-space truck terminal at the West Memphis interchange. For your logistics operation, it means relay staging, driver swap points, and equipment positioning right at the junction of I-40 and I-55 — 20 miles from Tulane Road. Instead of routing trucks to truck stops or back to Memphis for staging, you have terminal-quality infrastructure at the interchange itself.

For a carrier running HAZMAT loads to chemical customers across 48 states, staging flexibility at the Mid-South's busiest interchange tightens your routing options considerably.

Worth 20 minutes to walk through what it looks like for Empire's lanes?

JJ
drafts.hook_summary: Logistics/routing impact — staging and relay at the interchange
drafts.notes: JJ sends Day 2. Logistics angle — different from COO, Safety, and Maintenance pitches. Jonathan manages freight flow. Pending email verification.

drafts.subject: Fleet maintenance at I-40/I-55 — for Empire's 152 trucks
drafts.body:
Tommy —

I know Empire inspects every truck that rolls through the Memphis terminal. That kind of maintenance discipline is what keeps a HAZMAT fleet running safely.

We're building flex-space truck terminal infrastructure at the I-40/I-55 interchange in West Memphis — about 20 miles from Tulane Road. For your fleet, that means professional maintenance, truck wash, and secure equipment parking at the first major interchange west of Memphis. When your trucks are heading down I-40 and need service, the option is no longer a truck stop or turning around.

For 152 trucks running 48 states, having a maintenance-quality facility 20 miles from your yard — without building or leasing it — changes the equation.

Can we talk through what this looks like for Empire's fleet operations?

JJ
drafts.hook_summary: Fleet maintenance extension — Tulane Road standard at the interchange
drafts.ask: Call to discuss fleet operations impact
drafts.notes: JJ sends Day 2. Fleet Manager angle — parallel to Danny Walston email but at the implementation level. Tommy reports to Danny. If both respond, JJ references the other conversation to build momentum.

drafts.subject: Following up — flex-space truck terminal for Empire's drivers
drafts.body:
Rod —

Following up on our conversation. Here's the overview of what we're building at the I-40/I-55 interchange for carriers like Empire Express.

LineHaul Station is a flex-space truck terminal — not a truck stop. Your drivers get professional staging, maintenance, truck wash, secure parking, and quality rest facilities at the busiest freight interchange in the Mid-South. For Empire's HAZMAT drivers, this is especially relevant: quality rest and safe staging for loaded chemical trailers isn't optional.

Dedicated Proprietary membership: $100,000 initiation (80% refundable) + $13 per day. Terminal-quality infrastructure for less than a truck stop overnight. The site is 20 miles from Tulane Road.

When can we get 20 minutes to walk through how this works for your driver operations?

JJ

For your FedEx air cargo feeder lanes, LHS at the interchange is a staging point that tightens time windows and reduces variance. For your chemical shipper accounts, it's an infrastructure story that most carriers can't match.

LineHaul Station is a flex-space truck terminal 20 miles from Tulane Road. Dedicated Proprietary access: $100,000 initiation (80% refundable) + $13/day.

Jera —

Empire was built to attract drivers who want to be treated right. That story gets drivers in the door. LHS founding membership gives you something to put on the job posting that most carriers can't offer: Empire drivers get dedicated terminal infrastructure at the I-40/I-55 interchange — staging, maintenance, truck wash, secure rest facilities — not truck stops.

For CDL-A HAZMAT-certified drivers who have options, that's a real differentiator. And for retention: LHS at $13/day costs less than the first week of a driver replacement.

drafts.subject: Following up — flex-space truck terminals at the interchange

Tim —

I reached out last week about what we're building at the I-40/I-55 interchange. I know you're running 152 trucks and don't have time for pitches — so I'll keep this simple.

Flex-space truck terminal infrastructure. 20 miles from Tulane Road. Driver staging, maintenance, truck wash, secure HAZMAT parking. $100K initiation, 80% refundable, $13/day. No construction. No lease. Founding member access.

Your dad built Empire Express for drivers. We're building infrastructure for the same reason. I'd like to show you the site — 20-minute drive from your yard.

Jeff
